description: Study 2: input-framing probe. 3 problems x 3 framings (prose / nudge / Lean-stub) x 3 runs = 27 unattended attempts, counting tool calls by type.
motivation: Test whether the zero-adoption result is an artifact of input framing: does Lean-syntax framing (or a soft nudge) pull in math-viz / math-compute that prose framing does not?
formal_results: math-viz invoked 0 times across all 27 runs, in every framing. Mean total tool calls: prose 9.0, nudge 10.9, Lean 7.3 (Lean framing explored the LEAST). math-compute small and not elevated under Lean.
results_description: Framing does not move viz adoption off zero; Lean-stub framing actually reduced overall tool reach.
conclusions: The input-framing hypothesis is refuted: framing is not the lever for visualization adoption.
